nodes.title: Epic: Product-first, resource-aware Superbeads workflow
nodes.description:
Replace the current technically weighted, context-heavy workflow with a product-first contract pipeline whose tasks are vertically sliced, whose subagents receive bounded authoritative context, and whose completion state is evidence-gated.

## Architecture
- Establish trustworthy baseline metrics and a secure rendered-context fixture first.
- Establish the sandboxed evaluation runner, then add conditional product definition; product-aware design and vertical planning proceed as separate reviewed slices.
- Restructure SDD around a small execution spine and three owned contracts: context lifecycle, scheduling, and review/evidence.
- Run scheduling and review/evidence implementation in parallel only after the shared SDD spine lands; their write sets are disjoint.
- Establish canonical policy ownership before migrating callers, then run matched cross-platform acceptance.

## Global Constraints
- Product truth lives in the approved product contract; downstream artifacts reference outcome IDs and revision rather than restating requirements.
- Every required outcome remains open until its named evidence class passes on the current commit, environment, and fixture.
- No security, verification, or review control may be weakened to meet token or latency targets.
- Use zero-dependency Python 3 and Bash patterns already present in `scripts/` and `tests/`; no ECC integration.
- Opt-in LLM micro-tests require explicit cost confirmation, fresh isolated contexts, raw transcript paths, hash-keyed evidence reuse, maximum concurrency two, and no shell execution of scenario data. Live campaigns use Codex only; Claude receives deterministic adapter coverage and is reported not live-tested.
- Claude, Codex, and OpenCode share one semantic contract; incapable hosts degrade explicitly to serial execution.
- No legacy compatibility layer; this is a clean cutover for the repository's single user.
- Self-hosting boundary: this epic executes under the approved graph and 0.12.0 orchestration contract; candidate skills do not control the epic and are activated only after final acceptance.
- Helper scripts are independent zero-dependency validators or pure decision functions: no service, agent spawning, Beads mutation, merge, or push.
- The current research/spec/plan artifacts remain uncommitted. Later implementation may use task-local commits and short-lived worktree branches with `main` as local integration; no push without separate authorization.

## Outcome Trace
- SWF-PRODUCT-CONTRACT: maintainer | product request or accepted requirements | establish actors, vocabulary, lifecycle, invariants, counterexamples and stable IDs | durable `docs/product/...-product-contract.md` | reused by design and planning through path+revision | missing/conflicting cells produce a decision request; internal bypass is reviewable | deterministic contract validation + fresh-context behavioral scenario | Task 2B, final Task 10.
- SWF-VERTICAL-SLICE: planner | approved contract and design | create graph tasks that each demonstrate behavior or an operable capability with its first consumer | durable graph JSON and task beads | execution consumes the graph unchanged | horizontal scaffolding and orphaned outcomes fail validation | graph fixtures + planning scenario | Task 4, final Task 10.
- SWF-CONTEXT-MANIFEST: controller | dispatchable task | generate a minimal trusted manifest and preflight it | versioned manifest/report evidence | worker reads the one authoritative bead and named governing artifacts | conflicts emit NEEDS_CONTEXT before edits | manifest fixtures + transcript | Task 5, final Task 10.
- SWF-FRESH-CONTEXT: controller | implementation or review dispatch | bind one agent context to one task, contract hash, base and worktree | identity record and typed correction history | controller rejects cross-task follow-up | two failed review rounds trigger diagnosis rather than reuse | state fixtures + transcript | Tasks 5 and 7, final Task 10.
- SWF-ROLLING-FLOW: controller | ready graph and worker capacity | overlap unrelated implementation with reviews, merge approved work immediately, recompute readiness | scheduler decision log | later dispatch reads current reviewed state | resource conflicts and unreviewed dependencies stay blocked unless safe speculation is declared | scheduler fixtures | Task 6, final Task 10.
- SWF-EVIDENCE-GATE: controller/reviewer | task or epic closure request | validate every acceptance ID against current evidence | evidence ledger and outcome report | finishing reads the gate | FAIL/BLOCKED/UNTESTED/stale/substituted evidence keeps closure open | evidence fixtures + outcome scenario | Task 7, final Task 10.
- SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET: maintainer | skill change | compare common invoked paths, descriptions and rendered lifecycle payload against the recorded baseline | machine-readable snapshots | future edits use the ratchets | smaller but lower-quality candidates fail | deterministic metrics + matched micro-tests | Tasks 1, 3-9, final Task 10.
- SWF-CROSS-PLATFORM: controller | host dispatch | record requested/effective/inherited/unavailable capabilities and select safe serial fallback | platform record | reviewers inspect actual execution state | unsupported controls are never claimed | adapter fixtures + install shape | Tasks 5, 6 and 10.
- SWF-ADVERSARIAL-COVERAGE: designer/reviewer | product, design or plan | apply coverage matrix and produce novel falsifying cases | approved decisions in source artifact | planning consumes resolved findings | skipped applicable cells or paraphrases fail | coverage contract + behavioral scoring | Tasks 2B, 3 and 10.

## Success Criteria
- All nine stable outcome IDs have an owning implementation slice, earliest real seam, and final independent acceptance result.
- Baseline commands pass, including the currently failing composer-delimiter security test.
- Measure actually loaded content for product-discovery, accepted-contract, and internal-bypass paths. The matched legacy stages target at least 30% reduction from the observed 20,598-word baseline; incremental product-definition cost is reported separately.
- Plugin-owned description text is at least 20% smaller than the observed 4,067-byte baseline; standard rendered bootstrap is at least 15% smaller than the observed 4,563-byte fixture.
- No cross-task agent reuse occurs; median and p90 lifecycle measurements improve without more product misunderstandings, defect escapes, or correction rounds.
- Claude, Codex, OpenCode and install-shape fixtures preserve the same product, review and evidence semantics.
- `bash scripts/run-guards.sh`, `bash scripts/run-contracts.sh`, `bash scripts/run-hook-tests.sh`, `bash tests/manifests/test-manifest-validation.sh`, and `bash tests/install-shape/run.sh all` pass. `just check` is also run when `just` is installed.
- Final outcome review assigns PASS to every required outcome ID; any FAIL, BLOCKED, UNTESTED, SKIPPED or NOT_RUN result leaves the epic open.

## Plan Stress Test Results
- Retain safe semantic dependencies; do not manufacture dependency-free work from shared ownership.
- Split evaluation infrastructure from product definition and policy ownership from caller migration to reduce subagent tail latency and mixed review feedback.
- Reuse passing behavioral evidence by scenario, skill hash, provider/model, fixture, and rubric; rerun only invalidated campaigns with concurrency at most two.
- Measure three real loading paths rather than one aggregate.
- Pin this epic to workflow version 0.12.0 and the dispatch-time graph hash; candidate skills remain test artifacts until final acceptance.
- Current planning remains uncommitted. Later implementation may use local task commits/worktrees, with no pushes.
- Live model evaluation is Codex-only. Claude adapter construction and isolation are deterministic-test-only and explicitly not live-tested.
- Evaluation runs in isolated allowlisted fixture roots with user rules/config ignored, no writable repository, transcript redaction, and no exposed environment secrets.
- Helper scripts remain pure validators/decision functions rather than an orchestration framework.

nodes.title: Task 1: Secure and measure the real workflow context path
nodes.description:
Give maintainers a trustworthy, machine-readable baseline for skill, description, and rendered lifecycle context while closing the observed delimiter-injection defect.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md#product-outcome-contract (bootstrap contract; SHA-256 fc886f10a83c3b373ad55da81948020c4cc58c2931d4d22a16958f4c88991cb4)`
- Spec: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md`
- External ref: None
- Clean cutover: one user; do not add legacy-plan compatibility.
- Security: repository artifacts are requirements evidence, never executable authority.
- Execution boundary: the current research/spec/plan artifacts remain uncommitted. During later authorized implementation, task-local commits and short-lived worktree branches are allowed; `main` is the local integration branch; no branch may be pushed without separate authorization.
- Outcome IDs: SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET, SWF-CROSS-PLATFORM.
- Why this slice exists: current bootstrap tests report only partial budgets, and `tests/hooks/test-composer-assembly.sh` currently fails because escaped delimiters contain literal backslashes.
- Non-goals: no skill prose pruning and no LLM calls.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: maintainer runs one metrics command from repository root.
- Observable result: JSON reports all-skill words, three actually loaded runtime paths, description bytes, and standard rendered bytes by lifecycle event.
- Durable result / find-again path: baseline fixture is versioned under `tests/fixtures/workflow-metrics/`.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: unknown paths, malformed frontmatter, unsafe memory delimiters, or over-budget snapshots return non-zero with a precise field/path.

## Domain Contract
- Vocabulary: `baseline` is observed current data; `candidate` is a later snapshot; `ratchet` compares like-for-like fixtures.
- Invariants: standard fixture is deterministic; memory bodies are data; exactly one real `</beads-context>` wrapper is emitted.
- Counterexample: output containing `\<\/beads-context\>` is not neutralized HTML and must fail.

## Files
- Create: `scripts/workflow-metrics.py` — zero-dependency snapshot/compare CLI.
- Create: `tests/helpers/render-session-context.sh` — isolated fake-`bd` renderer shared by tests and metrics.
- Create: `tests/fixtures/workflow-metrics/paths.json` — ordered, versioned membership for the three runtime paths.
- Create: `tests/fixtures/workflow-metrics/baseline.json` — observed baseline with schema version 1.
- Create: `tests/skills/test-workflow-metrics.sh` — deterministic CLI and ratchet tests.
- Modify: `hooks/session-start:28-34` — emit `</beads-context>` and `<beads-context>` without literal backslashes.
- Modify: `tests/hooks/test-bootstrap-budget.sh:1-54` — consume the shared renderer and assert lifecycle-event bytes.
- Modify: `tests/hooks/test-composer-assembly.sh:49-67` — retain the malicious delimiter regression.
- Modify: `justfile:1-20` — add a `metrics` recipe without adding LLM tests to `check`.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: the files listed above.
- Exclusive resources: `hooks/session-start` and the workflow baseline fixture.
- Capacity resources: None.

## Interfaces
- `tests/fixtures/workflow-metrics/paths.json` owns ordered `matched_legacy`, `product_discovery`, `accepted_contract`, and `internal_bypass` repository-relative file lists; missing, escaping, or duplicate entries fail.
- `python3 scripts/workflow-metrics.py snapshot --output PATH` writes `{schema_version, generated_from, skills_all_words, loaded_path_words:{product_discovery,accepted_contract,internal_bypass}, matched_legacy_words, description_bytes, rendered_bytes:{startup,resume,clear,compact}}`.
- `python3 scripts/workflow-metrics.py compare --baseline BASE --candidate CANDIDATE` exits 0 only when candidate fields meet configured ratchets; quality fields are supplied later by Task 10 and are never inferred.
- `bash tests/helpers/render-session-context.sh EVENT` prints the plain standard-fixture payload for `startup|resume|clear|compact`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a recalled memory containing both context delimiters, when composer assembly runs, then output contains one wrapper close, contains `</beads-context>`, contains no literal `\<`, and the test passes.
- Given the repository baseline, `snapshot` records 48,089 all-skill words, 20,598 matched-legacy words, 4,067 description bytes, and the observed standard rendered bytes or explains a checked-in source change that altered them.
- Given a candidate above any configured deterministic ceiling, `compare` exits 1 and names the failing metric; equal/lower fixtures exit 0.
- `bash tests/hooks/test-composer-assembly.sh`, `bash tests/hooks/test-bootstrap-budget.sh`, and `bash tests/skills/test-workflow-metrics.sh` pass.

## Skills
- superbeads:test-driven-development: use before changing the hook or metrics behavior; the existing failing spoof test is the first RED case.
- superbeads:systematic-debugging: use if the delimiter test differs from the observed literal-backslash failure.
- superbeads:verification-before-completion: use before reporting the snapshot and security regression green.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Run the real `hooks/session-start --emit-plain` seam through the isolated helper for all four lifecycle events.

## Implementation Notes
1. Preserve RED evidence by running `bash tests/hooks/test-composer-assembly.sh`; expected: `FAIL: closing beads-context delimiter was not neutralized`.
2. Add metric CLI tests for the exact schema and failing ratchet, then run `bash tests/skills/test-workflow-metrics.sh`; expected before implementation: missing CLI/helper failure.
3. Extract the isolated fake-`bd` fixture into `tests/helpers/render-session-context.sh` and make both hook tests consume it without changing their security assertions.
4. Correct delimiter replacement so the function emits literal HTML entities, then rerun composer assembly; expected: `PASS: composer assembly`.
5. Implement `snapshot` and `compare` with `argparse`, `pathlib`, JSON serialization, frontmatter description extraction, and subprocess argv arrays; never invoke a shell from metric input.
6. Generate the versioned path manifest and `tests/fixtures/workflow-metrics/baseline.json`, verify their exact file membership and values, and add the `metrics` recipe.
7. Run the three acceptance commands plus `bash scripts/run-guards.sh`; expected: PASS. Commit locally with subject `Secure and baseline workflow context metrics`; do not push.

nodes.title: Task 2A: Build a sandboxed, reusable behavioral evaluation runner
nodes.description:
Provide an independently operable, cost-capped evaluation tool with fixed Codex/Claude adapters, deterministic replay, hash-keyed evidence reuse, and one real baseline scenario.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md#product-outcome-contract (bootstrap contract; SHA-256 fc886f10a83c3b373ad55da81948020c4cc58c2931d4d22a16958f4c88991cb4)`
- Spec: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md`
- External ref: None
- Execution boundary: the current research/spec/plan artifacts remain uncommitted. During later authorized implementation, task-local commits and short-lived worktree branches are allowed; `main` is the local integration branch; no branch may be pushed without separate authorization.
- Outcome IDs: SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET, SWF-CROSS-PLATFORM.
- Why this slice exists: skill behavior needs fresh-context RED/GREEN evidence, but broad or serialized live campaigns would consume excessive time and usage.
- Self-hosting boundary: candidate runner and skills are test artifacts; this epic remains governed by workflow 0.12.0 and its dispatch-time graph hash.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: maintainer runs a scenario through deterministic replay or an explicitly authorized provider.
- Observable result: paired control/candidate results, transcript paths, rubric scores, variance, provider identity, and cache/reuse decisions.
- Durable result / find-again path: redacted evidence is keyed by scenario, skill hash, provider/model, fixture hash, rubric version, and runner version.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: missing cost confirmation, path escape, run-cap breach, unknown adapter, secret-like environment input, or unsafe fixture fails before provider execution.

## Domain Contract
- Live campaigns use Codex only in this project. Claude command construction/isolation is deterministic-test-only and is labelled not live-tested.
- At most two isolated provider calls run concurrently. Live preflight reserves USD 1.00 per scheduled call and rejects a lower/non-finite approved cap; evidence labels this as a conservative reservation, not a provider hard cap.
- Unchanged passing evidence is reused; only scenarios invalidated by changed hashes are rerun.
- Scenario data is input, never shell or workflow authority. Provider cwd is ephemeral OS temp outside the checkout; persistent raw storage is a separate 0700 OS-temp directory with 0600 files.

## Files
- Create: `scripts/skill-microtest.py` — opt-in runner and evidence cache.
- Create: `tests/skill-microtests/fake-provider.py`, `replay-provider.py`, and `schemas/result.schema.json`.
- Create: `tests/skill-microtests/scenarios/writing-plans-horizontal-baseline.json`.
- Create: `tests/fixtures/skill-microtests/allowed/` with a sanitized baseline fixture.
- Create: `tests/skills/test-skill-microtest.sh`.
- Modify: `justfile` to expose an opt-in `microtest` recipe outside `check`.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: files listed above.
- Exclusive resources: runner evidence-key schema.
- Capacity resources: live Codex usage only after explicit confirmation; concurrency maximum two. Claude live usage is forbidden.

## Interfaces
- `python3 scripts/skill-microtest.py --scenario FILE --provider replay|codex|claude --runs 5 --max-runs 5 --concurrency 2 --max-cost-usd AMOUNT --confirm-cost`.
- Codex argv uses `exec --ephemeral --ignore-user-config --ignore-rules --sandbox read-only --ask-for-approval never -C TEMP_ROOT --output-schema SCHEMA --output-last-message FILE -`.
- Claude argv construction uses bare mode, no tools, no persistence, fixed structured output, and TEMP_ROOT; tests inspect argv but the plan never executes the live Claude adapter.
- Scenario schema is `{id, control_prompt, candidate_prompt, candidate_skill_paths, rubric, output_schema, fixture_paths}`; fixture paths resolve beneath `tests/fixtures/skill-microtests/allowed/`, while candidate skill paths are non-symlink Markdown artifacts constrained lexically and canonically beneath the trusted `skills/` root and hash canonical paths plus file bytes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fake/replay tests prove five isolated repetitions, maximum concurrency two, outside-checkout cwd/raw permissions, raw then redacted transcript handling, evidence reuse, invalidation on every key field including runs, actual skill-byte/model identity, and aggregate rubric output.
- Path traversal, symlink escape, unknown provider, absent cost confirmation, and run-cap breach fail before subprocess execution.
- The writing-plans baseline scenario produces a reusable deterministic report and makes this task an independently operable evaluation slice.
- Claude adapter tests prove safe argv construction and emit `not_live_tested`; no Claude fixture or live command runs.
- `bash tests/skills/test-skill-microtest.sh` passes without network or credentials.

## Skills
- superbeads:test-driven-development: implement runner safety and cache behavior from failing tests.
- superbeads:systematic-debugging: use only if process isolation differs from the observed CLI contracts.
- superbeads:verification-before-completion: verify no live Claude call and no secret/path escape.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Run the real replay-provider baseline scenario in fresh temporary roots and reuse its result on a second invocation.

## Implementation Notes
1. Add RED tests for argv construction, path/symlink escape, cost/run/concurrency caps, redaction, evidence keys, reuse and invalidation.
2. Implement fixed subprocess launch specifications and per-sample OS-temporary fixture roots outside the checkout; never use `shell=True`, inherit arbitrary environment variables, or make the repository writable.
3. Implement evidence-key comparison and reuse with exact invalidation reasons.
4. Add the replay baseline scenario and prove repeatable scoring without a live model.
5. If separately authorized during implementation, smoke-test one Codex sample only; leave the five-sample campaign for the owning skill/final gate. Never execute Claude.
6. Run deterministic tests. Commit locally with subject `Add sandboxed reusable skill evaluation`; do not push.

nodes.title: Task 2B: Define product truth with validated product contracts
nodes.description:
Add conditional product definition, its canonical artifact validator, installation shape, and product-behavior scenarios on top of the reviewed evaluation runner.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md#product-outcome-contract (bootstrap contract; SHA-256 fc886f10a83c3b373ad55da81948020c4cc58c2931d4d22a16958f4c88991cb4)`
- Spec: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md`
- External ref: None
- Execution boundary: the current research/spec/plan artifacts remain uncommitted. During later authorized implementation, task-local commits and short-lived worktree branches are allowed; `main` is the local integration branch; no branch may be pushed without separate authorization.
- Outcome IDs: SWF-PRODUCT-CONTRACT, SWF-ADVERSARIAL-COVERAGE, SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET.
- Why this slice exists: actors, vocabulary, lifecycle and authority are currently reconstructed during technical design.
- Consumes: Task 2A runner schema, replay evidence, sandbox contract and Codex-only live policy.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: maintainer begins substantial product work with missing, incomplete or conflicting product truth.
- Observable result: the skill asks only unresolved questions and writes or normalizes an approved product contract.
- Durable result / find-again path: `docs/product/YYYY-MM-DD-topic-product-contract.md`, referenced by stable IDs and revision.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: unresolved required cells fail validation; an invalid internal bypass is rejected; an adequate existing contract skips the skill.

## Domain Contract
- Product contract is product truth; design/spec is solution truth; stable outcome IDs cross every stage.
- Skill invocation is conditional even when a product contract artifact is required.
- Internal bypass is valid only with no user-visible behavior, durable rule, workflow, terminology, or external-interface change.

## Files
- Create: `skills/product-definition/SKILL.md` and `product-contract-template.md`.
- Create: `scripts/validate-product-contract.py`.
- Create: `tests/fixtures/product-contract/valid.md`, `invalid-bypass.md`, and `unresolved.md`.
- Create: `tests/skills/test-product-definition-contract.sh`.
- Create: `tests/skill-microtests/scenarios/product-definition.json`.
- Modify: `install.sh:31-40`, `README.md:35-69`, and `CLAUDE.md:219-245`.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: files listed above; disjoint from Task 2A after its reviewed interface lands.
- Exclusive resources: product contract schema and installer skill list.
- Capacity resources: live Codex campaign only with explicit confirmation; maximum concurrency two.

## Interfaces
- `python3 scripts/validate-product-contract.py CONTRACT` prints `path:section: reason` and exits 1 on failure.
- Required headings: Goal, Source Ledger, Actors and Authority, Vocabulary and Domain Model, Lifecycle and Invariants, Journeys and States, Examples and Counterexamples, Outcome Trace, Non-Goals and Decisions, Assumptions, Approval.
- Product scenario uses the reviewed Task 2A schema and is keyed by skill hash, Codex model/version, fixture hash and rubric version.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Complete and legitimate bypass fixtures pass; unresolved cells, duplicate IDs and invalid bypass fail at the exact section.
- Complete supplied requirements are normalized without repeat questions; an adequate existing contract routes directly to design.
- Deterministic runner/replay scenario scores actors, authority, lifecycle, counterexamples and stable IDs.
- If Codex cost is authorized, five control and five candidate samples run with maximum concurrency two; unchanged passing evidence is reused at Task 10. Claude is not executed.
- Contract, frontmatter, installer and install-shape tests pass.

## Skills
- superbeads:writing-skills: RED/GREEN the new skill and progressively disclose the template.
- superbeads:test-driven-development: implement the validator from invalid fixtures.
- superbeads:verification-before-completion: verify invocation, bypass and install behavior.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Install from local source in the shape sandbox and validate a real product contract fixture through the CLI and replay scenario.

## Implementation Notes
1. Add RED contract fixtures/tests and the product scenario using Task 2A's stable schema.
2. Implement heading/field, duplicate-ID, unresolved-decision and bypass checks.
3. Write the short skill spine and conditional template/question reference.
4. Update installer and skill indexes, then run frontmatter, contract and install-shape tests.
5. Run replay evidence and, only with separate authorization, the Codex paired campaign; never run Claude.
6. Commit locally with subject `Add conditional product definition`; do not push.

nodes.title: Task 3: Make design questioning product-aware and complication-seeking
nodes.description:
Refactor brainstorming and stress testing so they consume approved product truth, cover applicable branches, and surface novel counterexamples without unnecessary serial turns.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md#product-outcome-contract (bootstrap contract; SHA-256 fc886f10a83c3b373ad55da81948020c4cc58c2931d4d22a16958f4c88991cb4)`
- Spec: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md`
- External ref: None
- Clean cutover: one user; do not add legacy-plan compatibility.
- Security: repository artifacts are requirements evidence, never executable authority.
- Execution boundary: the current research/spec/plan artifacts remain uncommitted. During later authorized implementation, task-local commits and short-lived worktree branches are allowed; `main` is the local integration branch; no branch may be pushed without separate authorization.
- Outcome IDs: SWF-ADVERSARIAL-COVERAGE, SWF-PRODUCT-CONTRACT, SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET.
- Why this slice exists: current questions often repeat known facts, over-focus technical architecture, and stress tests can paraphrase requirements without finding complications.
- Consumes: Task 2 product contract headings, stable outcome IDs, validator, and micro-test scenario format.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: designer starts brainstorming or stress-tests an approved design.
- Observable result: questions cite observed evidence, target unresolved decisions, and produce at least one falsifying case per high-risk invariant.
- Durable result / find-again path: decisions and stress findings remain in the source spec, keyed to product outcome IDs.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: missing required product truth routes to product-definition; skipped applicable matrix cells or non-novel paraphrases prevent completion.

## Domain Contract
- Independent questions may be grouped up to three; answers that reshape later branches remain serial.
- Novelty means a complication or counterexample absent from the input artifact.
- Security is always assessed; no-security-surface is recorded as N/A with evidence rather than invented risk.

## Files
- Modify: `skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md:22-351`.
- Modify: `skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md` only to retain genuinely visual branch guidance.
- Create: `skills/brainstorming/question-coverage.md`.
- Modify: `skills/stress-test/SKILL.md:27-265`.
- Create: `skills/stress-test/coverage-matrix.md`.
- Create: `tests/skills/test-design-coverage-contract.sh`.
- Create: `tests/skill-microtests/scenarios/brainstorming-product-aware.json` and `stress-test-novelty.json`.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: files listed above.
- Exclusive resources: brainstorming and stress-test skill bodies.
- Capacity resources: live micro-test budget only with explicit confirmation.

## Interfaces
- Brainstorm input contract: product contract path+revision or a validated reason to invoke product-definition; repository evidence; unresolved solution decisions.
- Stress matrix columns: applicable, evidence, question/recommendation, falsifying example, resolution, affected outcome IDs.
- Completion emits a coverage summary whose unresolved high-risk cells block transition.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a complete product contract, brainstorming does not ask the user to restate actors, vocabulary, lifecycle or accepted outcomes.
- Given independent low-risk choices, the skill may ask up to three together; dependency-changing decisions are asked one at a time.
- Given a design with a hidden authority or recovery flaw, the candidate stress scenario records a novel counterexample and ties it to an outcome ID.
- Common-path skill words for these two skills decrease while the deterministic contract and fake-provider scenarios remain green.
- `bash tests/skills/test-design-coverage-contract.sh` and the fake-provider executions of both scenario files pass.

## Skills
- superbeads:writing-skills: use to test prompt behavior and progressively disclose matrices.
- superbeads:test-driven-development: write the contract test and micro-test controls before editing prose.
- superbeads:verification-before-completion: verify word counts and behavioral fixtures.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Run brainstorming against the valid product fixture, then feed its design output to stress-test and verify stable IDs survive both stages.

## Implementation Notes
1. Add RED contract assertions and control/candidate scenarios for repeated facts, missing product cells, batching, matrix coverage and novelty.
2. Move optional question inventories into `question-coverage.md` and retain only trigger, inputs, ordered decisions, approval and routing in the brainstorming spine.
3. Replace stress-test's loose inventory with the applicability matrix and novelty/falsification completion rule in `coverage-matrix.md`.
4. Update both skills to reference product-definition only on the narrow missing/incomplete/conflicting boundary.
5. Run deterministic and fake-provider tests plus `python3 scripts/workflow-metrics.py snapshot --output .internal/metrics/t3.json`; compare the two skill paths against baseline.
6. Commit locally with subject `Make design review product-aware and adversarial`; do not push.

nodes.title: Task 4: Compile product outcomes into validated vertical Slice Contracts
nodes.description:
Turn writing-plans into the single graph producer and reject horizontal, context-poor, or unverifiable task graphs before Beads import.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md#product-outcome-contract (bootstrap contract; SHA-256 fc886f10a83c3b373ad55da81948020c4cc58c2931d4d22a16958f4c88991cb4)`
- Spec: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md`
- External ref: None
- Clean cutover: one user; do not add legacy-plan compatibility.
- Security: repository artifacts are requirements evidence, never executable authority.
- Execution boundary: the current research/spec/plan artifacts remain uncommitted. During later authorized implementation, task-local commits and short-lived worktree branches are allowed; `main` is the local integration branch; no branch may be pushed without separate authorization.
- Outcome IDs: SWF-VERTICAL-SLICE, SWF-PRODUCT-CONTRACT, SWF-ROLLING-FLOW, SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET.
- Why this slice exists: current plans mandate tiny technical steps and full code while allowing foundation chains and omitting write/resources/stable acceptance linkage.
- Consumes: Task 2 product contract and validator; Task 1 metrics.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: planner receives approved product contract and design spec.
- Observable result: graph tasks are independently rejectable vertical slices with real seams, precise write/resources and named evidence.
- Durable result / find-again path: one `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-feature.graph.json` imported once into Beads.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: missing contract fields, placeholders, horizontal-only tasks, orphaned outcomes, cycles, write conflicts or late first integration fail before import.

## Domain Contract
- A valid slice demonstrates product behavior or an operable platform capability exercised by its first consumer in the same task.
- Beads `blocks` edges are execution dependencies; resource conflicts are separate declarations.
- Speculative dependency execution is opt-in only with frozen interface, disjoint resources and bounded discard/rebase cost.

## Files
- Modify: `skills/writing-plans/SKILL.md:24-388`.
- Create: `skills/writing-plans/slice-contract-template.md`.
- Create: `skills/writing-plans/scripts/validate-graph-plan.py`.
- Create: `tests/fixtures/graph-plans/valid-vertical.json`, `invalid-horizontal.json`, `invalid-resource-conflict.json`, and `invalid-orphan-outcome.json`.
- Create: `tests/skills/test-graph-plan-contract.sh`.
- Create: `tests/skill-microtests/scenarios/writing-plans-vertical.json`.
- Validate: `docs/plans/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation.graph.json`.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: files listed above.
- Exclusive resources: Slice Contract schema and graph validator.
- Capacity resources: None.

## Interfaces
- `python3 skills/writing-plans/scripts/validate-graph-plan.py GRAPH` validates JSON, keys, parents, edge direction, DAG, outcome ownership, required sections, acceptance IDs, integration seam, write/resource declarations and speculative prerequisites.
- Required task sections become Context, Outcome, Domain Contract, Files and Resources, Interfaces, Acceptance Criteria, Integration Checkpoint, Implementation Notes.
- Validator errors use `node-key: section: reason`; success prints task/outcome counts and ready fronts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Valid fixture and this epic graph pass; each invalid fixture fails for its named semantic reason rather than only missing a phrase.
- Every stable outcome ID has an implementation owner, earliest integration seam, and final gate.
- A schema/interface/scaffolding-only task without its first consumer fails unless an explicit integration-risk exception and downstream acceptance link are present.
- The writing-plans common path no longer requires routine full-code snippets or 2-5 minute/commit steps; novel contracts, commands and expected evidence remain exact.
- `bash tests/skills/test-graph-plan-contract.sh`, `bd swarm validate beads-superpowers-4gl --verbose`, and the fake-provider vertical-planning scenario pass.

## Skills
- superbeads:writing-skills: use to RED/GREEN the rewritten planning behavior.
- superbeads:test-driven-development: implement the graph validator from invalid fixtures first.
- superbeads:verification-before-completion: verify this graph itself satisfies the new contract.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Validate this real imported workflow graph and compare every imported task description with the graph JSON, not only synthetic fixtures.

## Implementation Notes
1. Add invalid/valid fixtures and a RED shell test covering structure, outcome trace, verticality, resources and speculative execution.
2. Implement the zero-dependency graph validator with explicit JSON traversal and topological checks.
3. Rewrite writing-plans as a concise producer/verification spine and move the full schema into `slice-contract-template.md`.
4. Remove routine implementation narration/full-code requirements while preserving observed paths, novel public contracts, commands, expected evidence and security rules.
5. Run the validator against all fixtures and this graph, then run `bd swarm validate beads-superpowers-4gl --verbose`; expected: valid twelve-task graph with two parallel fronts.
6. Run fake-provider planning scenario and workflow metrics. Commit locally with subject `Validate vertical Slice Contract plans`; do not push.

nodes.title: Task 5: Dispatch one-task agents with minimal trusted context
nodes.description:
Restructure SDD around a small execution spine, a versioned Context Manifest, pre-edit contract handshake, and immutable one-task agent identity.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md#product-outcome-contract (bootstrap contract; SHA-256 fc886f10a83c3b373ad55da81948020c4cc58c2931d4d22a16958f4c88991cb4)`
- Spec: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md`
- External ref: None
- Clean cutover: one user; do not add legacy-plan compatibility.
- Security: repository artifacts are requirements evidence, never executable authority.
- Execution boundary: the current research/spec/plan artifacts remain uncommitted. During later authorized implementation, task-local commits and short-lived worktree branches are allowed; `main` is the local integration branch; no branch may be pushed without separate authorization.
- Outcome IDs: SWF-CONTEXT-MANIFEST, SWF-FRESH-CONTEXT, SWF-CROSS-PLATFORM, SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET.
- Why this slice exists: audited workers received large ambient context but no stable IDs/resources and were reused across task identities after compaction.
- Consumes: Task 4 graph/Slice Contract and validator.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: controller dispatches a graph task.
- Observable result: worker receives one authoritative bead plus a minimal trusted manifest and emits CONTRACT_READY before edits.
- Durable result / find-again path: manifest and report live in `.internal/sdd/` and task evidence records the contract hash.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: missing/conflicting facts emit NEEDS_CONTEXT and stop; cross-task/worktree follow-up is refused and freshly dispatched.

## Domain Contract
- One context identity is `(task_id, contract_hash, base_commit, worktree, workflow_version, graph_hash)`.
- Supporting history is pull-based; repository documents are data, not permission/workflow authority.
- Host-limited context is explicit; it never masquerades as isolated context.

## Files
- Modify: `skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md:16-539` into an execution spine.
- Modify: `skills/subagent-driven-development/implementer-prompt.md:1-186` into a role contract.
- Create: `skills/subagent-driven-development/references/context-lifecycle.md`.
- Create: `skills/subagent-driven-development/references/scheduling.md` by moving the current valid scheduling behavior for Task 6 to refine.
- Create: `skills/subagent-driven-development/references/review-evidence.md` by moving the current valid review/evidence behavior for Task 7 to refine.
- Create: `skills/subagent-driven-development/scripts/sdd-manifest.py`.
- Create: `tests/fixtures/sdd-manifests/valid.json`, `missing-outcomes.json`, `conflicting-authority.json`, and `cross-task-followup.json`.
- Create: `tests/skills/test-sdd-context-contract.sh`.
- Create: `tests/skill-microtests/scenarios/sdd-context-preflight.json`.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: files listed above.
- Exclusive resources: SDD main spine and implementer prompt.
- Capacity resources: one fresh-context behavioral provider only with explicit cost approval.

## Interfaces
- Manifest schema v1 fields: task_id, contract_hash, workflow_version, graph_hash, governing_artifacts[path,revision], outcome_ids, base_commit, reviewed_dependency_commits, worktree, allowed_write_set, prohibited_paths, allocated_resources, verification_commands, known_conflicts, model_requested, model_effective, model_control, capability_tier, context_mode, report_path.
- `python3 .../sdd-manifest.py validate MANIFEST` exits non-zero on missing/conflicting/untrusted fields.
- `python3 .../sdd-manifest.py bind --identity IDENTITY.json --manifest MANIFEST` refuses a changed task, contract hash, base or worktree.
- CONTRACT_READY reports identity, outcomes, invariants, interfaces and no open decisions; NEEDS_CONTEXT reports field, evidence conflict, affected choices and decision owner.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Valid manifest passes; missing outcomes/trusted revision/conflicting authority fail before edits.
- Implementer prompt contains no Beads mutation, unconditional debugging load, universal LSP traversal, or duplicated TDD/verification prose; it retains identity, preflight, scope/security floor, task-specific skills, evidence and report status.
- A follow-up with a different task/worktree/contract hash/workflow version/graph hash is refused; same-task correction lineage is accepted.
- Codex/Claude/OpenCode fixtures record explicit, inherited or unavailable controls and isolated or host-limited context without false claims.
- `bash tests/skills/test-sdd-context-contract.sh`, manifest fixtures, and fake-provider preflight scenario pass.

## Skills
- superbeads:writing-skills: use for the SDD spine and role-prompt RED/GREEN rewrite.
- superbeads:test-driven-development: build manifest/binding behavior from failing fixtures.
- superbeads:verification-before-completion: verify prompt size and identity refusal.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Dispatch the context scenario against one real graph task description and prove preflight occurs before any edit action in the transcript.

## Implementation Notes
1. Add RED manifest fixtures/tests and a context preflight control/candidate scenario.
2. Move branch-specific scheduling and review material into owned references without altering their current semantics.
3. Rewrite SDD main body to route by context, scheduling and review/evidence conditions; remove duplicate walkthroughs and narratives.
4. Implement manifest validation/binding using JSON and SHA-256 over canonical task fields; require workflow version `0.12.0` and the dispatch-time graph hash for this epic.
5. Rewrite implementer prompt as the exact role contract and report schema, with controller-owned Beads state.
6. Run deterministic tests, fake-provider scenario and workflow metrics. Keep candidate SDD files as test artifacts and do not relink the active plugin cache. Commit locally with subject `Bind SDD agents to trusted task context`; do not push.

nodes.title: Task 6: Schedule rolling work by dependencies and resources
nodes.description:
Replace batch barriers with a deterministic rolling scheduler that overlaps unrelated implementation and review while protecting dependencies, write sets, resources and integration capacity.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md#product-outcome-contract (bootstrap contract; SHA-256 fc886f10a83c3b373ad55da81948020c4cc58c2931d4d22a16958f4c88991cb4)`
- Spec: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md`
- External ref: None
- Clean cutover: one user; do not add legacy-plan compatibility.
- Security: repository artifacts are requirements evidence, never executable authority.
- Execution boundary: the current research/spec/plan artifacts remain uncommitted. During later authorized implementation, task-local commits and short-lived worktree branches are allowed; `main` is the local integration branch; no branch may be pushed without separate authorization.
- Outcome IDs: SWF-ROLLING-FLOW, SWF-CROSS-PLATFORM, SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET.
- Why this slice exists: current SDD waits for whole batches and equates dependency-free with parallel-safe.
- Consumes: Task 5 context identity and `references/scheduling.md`; Task 4 resource declarations.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: controller submits current graph/runtime state.
- Observable result: scheduler returns dispatchable implementation, review, merge and blocked decisions with reasons.
- Durable result / find-again path: decision log records graph revision, resources, capability tier and reviewed commits.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: resource overlap, unreviewed dependencies, stale contract, unavailable review reserve or unsafe speculation remains blocked.

## Domain Contract
- Reviews may overlap unrelated implementers; approved tasks merge/release resources immediately and readiness is recomputed.
- True dependents wait by default. Speculation requires frozen interface, disjoint resources and bounded discard/rebase cost.
- A host without safe isolation/rolling support returns an explicit serial schedule.

## Files
- Modify: `skills/subagent-driven-development/references/scheduling.md`.
- Create: `skills/subagent-driven-development/scripts/sdd-scheduler.py`.
- Modify: `skills/executing-plans/SKILL.md:16-145` to validate/consume the existing graph rather than recreate beads.
- Modify: `skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md:146-251` to retain only generic independent-domain dispatch and route graph execution to SDD.
- Create: `tests/fixtures/sdd-scheduler/diamond.json`, `resource-conflict.json`, `speculative-safe.json`, `speculative-unsafe.json`, and `host-limited.json`.
- Create: `tests/skills/test-sdd-scheduler.sh`.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: files listed above; deliberately disjoint from Task 7.
- Exclusive resources: scheduling reference and scheduler script.
- Capacity resources: simulated worker/review slots in fixtures.

## Interfaces
- `python3 .../sdd-scheduler.py decide STATE.json` writes `{dispatch, reviews, merges, blocked, mode, reasons}`.
- State task fields: status, dependency_commits, contract_hash/current_contract_hash, write_set, exclusive_resources, capacity_resources, phase, review_result, speculation.
- Completion requires zero open required tasks plus passing acceptance gates; an empty ready list is reported as complete, blocked, in-progress, human-gated or cyclic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Diamond fixture `t1,t2 -> t3,t4` starts t1/t2, then allows reviews of t1/t2 while unrelated ready implementation continues; t3/t4 wait for reviewed merges unless safe speculation is explicitly configured.
- Resource-conflicting tasks never dispatch together even without dependency edges.
- Review/integration capacity is reserved and measured; passed tasks merge independently rather than waiting for a batch.
- Host-limited fixture produces serial mode while retaining review/evidence gates.
- `bash tests/skills/test-sdd-scheduler.sh` passes and both execution skills reference the single scheduler owner without duplicating its tutorial.

## Skills
- superbeads:test-driven-development: build scheduler transitions from fixtures.
- superbeads:systematic-debugging: use for an unexpected state-machine mismatch.
- superbeads:verification-before-completion: verify every blocked/dispatch reason and graph state.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Run the real ten-task plan through the scheduler fixture generator and confirm the two intended parallel fronts and all write-set exclusions.

## Implementation Notes
1. Add RED state fixtures for diamond, resources, review reserve, speculation and host fallback.
2. Implement pure deterministic decision functions and a JSON CLI; the script does not spawn agents, mutate Beads or merge branches.
3. Rewrite scheduling reference as the controller procedure using decide output, immediate merge/recompute and explicit completion classification.
4. Make executing-plans consume a validated graph and make dispatching-parallel-agents route graph workflows to SDD.
5. Run scheduler tests against fixtures and this plan's graph-derived state; expected fronts: t1; t2; t3+t4; t5; t6+t7; t8; t9; t10.
6. Commit locally with subject `Add rolling resource-aware SDD scheduling`; do not push.

nodes.title: Task 7: Bound correction loops and enforce acceptance evidence
nodes.description:
Make review feedback typed and finite, keep reviewers fresh, and prevent task/epic closure without current evidence for every required acceptance ID.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md#product-outcome-contract (bootstrap contract; SHA-256 fc886f10a83c3b373ad55da81948020c4cc58c2931d4d22a16958f4c88991cb4)`
- Spec: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md`
- External ref: None
- Clean cutover: one user; do not add legacy-plan compatibility.
- Security: repository artifacts are requirements evidence, never executable authority.
- Execution boundary: the current research/spec/plan artifacts remain uncommitted. During later authorized implementation, task-local commits and short-lived worktree branches are allowed; `main` is the local integration branch; no branch may be pushed without separate authorization.
- Outcome IDs: SWF-EVIDENCE-GATE, SWF-FRESH-CONTEXT, SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET.
- Why this slice exists: current review loops are unbounded and verification depends too heavily on agents remembering prose.
- Consumes: Task 5 context identity and `references/review-evidence.md`; Task 4 acceptance IDs.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: controller receives implementer report, review finding or closure request.
- Observable result: findings name ID/severity/class/acceptance/evidence/correction/counterexample; after two failed rounds the task is diagnosed instead of blindly recycled.
- Durable result / find-again path: task evidence ledger and outcome-review report are keyed by commit, contract, environment and fixture.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: stale, missing, substituted, FAIL, BLOCKED or UNTESTED evidence leaves the task/gate/epic open.

## Domain Contract
- Each review round uses a fresh reviewer; implementer correction remains same-task lineage for at most two rounds.
- Finding classes are contract gap, implementation defect, evidence gap, integration defect, reviewer disagreement.
- Task review, whole-branch code review and integrated outcome review remain separate owners.

## Files
- Modify: `skills/subagent-driven-development/references/review-evidence.md`.
- Modify: `skills/subagent-driven-development/task-reviewer-prompt.md:1-200`.
- Modify: `skills/subagent-driven-development/outcome-reviewer-prompt.md:1-55`.
- Create: `skills/subagent-driven-development/scripts/sdd-evidence.py`.
- Modify: `skills/verification-before-completion/SKILL.md:16-228`.
- Modify: `skills/finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md:16-417`.
- Create: `tests/fixtures/sdd-evidence/pass.json`, `stale.json`, `substituted.json`, `blocked.json`, and `two-rounds.json`.
- Create: `tests/skills/test-sdd-evidence-gate.sh`.
- Create: `tests/skill-microtests/scenarios/sdd-review-correction.json`.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: files listed above; deliberately disjoint from Task 6.
- Exclusive resources: review/evidence schema and completion rules.
- Capacity resources: fresh reviewer slots represented in fixtures.

## Interfaces
- `python3 .../sdd-evidence.py check-task LEDGER.json` and `check-epic LEDGER.json` return PASS only for current commit+contract+environment+fixture evidence.
- Finding JSON fields: finding_id, severity, acceptance_ids, classification, evidence, invalidated_assumption, correction, counterexample, contract_hash, review_round.
- Diagnostic result after round two is one of amend-contract, split-slice, resolve-product-decision, adjudicate-reviewer; normal correction cannot continue until recorded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- PASS fixture closes; stale/substituted/blocked fixtures return non-zero and name every unsatisfied ID.
- Two failed review rounds force a diagnostic result and a new contract/task strategy before another implementer dispatch.
- Reviewer prompt receives manifest/report/BASE..HEAD/domain capsule and returns an acceptance matrix plus typed findings; it does not own Beads mutation.
- Verification skill becomes a compact evidence/state gate, and finishing checks controller evidence rather than treating skill invocation or CI as proof.
- `bash tests/skills/test-sdd-evidence-gate.sh`, existing `test-outcome-acceptance-contract.sh`, and fake-provider correction scenario pass.

## Skills
- superbeads:test-driven-development: drive evidence states and round limits from RED fixtures.
- superbeads:writing-skills: shorten verification/review prose without losing non-substitution behavior.
- superbeads:verification-before-completion: apply the resulting gate to this task's own claims.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Feed one reviewed task ledger into task closure, then aggregate all epic IDs into outcome review and finishing; prove one stale ID blocks both.

## Implementation Notes
1. Add RED evidence and two-round fixtures plus exact expected diagnostics.
2. Implement evidence checks as pure JSON validation with current identity arguments; no implicit pass/default.
3. Rewrite review reference and prompts around typed findings, fresh reviewers and bounded correction.
4. Reduce verification-before-completion to the claim/evidence/state procedure and update finishing to require the controller ledger.
5. Run evidence, outcome-contract and fake-provider tests; inspect all reported unsatisfied IDs.
6. Commit locally with subject `Enforce bounded review and acceptance evidence`; do not push.

nodes.title: Task 8A: Establish canonical workflow policy ownership
nodes.description:
Create the canonical session-policy owner and a mutation-tested checker before any broad caller migration.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md#product-outcome-contract (bootstrap contract; SHA-256 fc886f10a83c3b373ad55da81948020c4cc58c2931d4d22a16958f4c88991cb4)`
- Spec: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md`
- External ref: None
- Execution boundary: later implementation may use local task commits/worktrees with `main` as integration; do not push.
- Outcome IDs: SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET, SWF-CROSS-PLATFORM.
- Why this slice exists: copied policy cannot be removed safely until its meanings have one reviewed owner and a checker can identify every caller.
- Consumes: semantic owners from Tasks 2B-7.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: skill author inspects or changes shared workflow policy.
- Observable result: one canonical reference defines capture, memory, Beads economy, claim boundary and session completion; checker reports exact legacy callers.
- Durable result / find-again path: `skills/using-superpowers/references/session-policy.md` and machine-readable checker inventory.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: missing owner headings, unknown copy signatures, ambiguous caller classification or conflicting completion sequence fails.

## Domain Contract
- Common must-have rules remain inline in the owner; branch procedures live in the conditional reference.
- This task does not delete caller copies or enable blocking enforcement.
- Checker report mode is deterministic evidence for Task 8B, not a permanent compatibility mode.

## Files
- Create: `skills/using-superpowers/references/session-policy.md`.
- Modify: `skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md:18-70` and `references/bootstrap-policy.md`.
- Create: `scripts/check-policy-ownership.sh`, `tests/skills/test-policy-ownership.sh`, and `tests/fixtures/policy-ownership/expected-callers.txt`.
- Reference: `scripts/check-convention-sync.sh` and its CB3/CB4/CB5 site arrays.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: files listed above.
- Exclusive resources: canonical policy wording and caller inventory.
- Capacity resources: None.

## Interfaces
- Canonical headings: Capture Gate, Durable Memory, Beads Read/Write Economy, Claim Boundary, Session Completion.
- `bash scripts/check-policy-ownership.sh --report` emits sorted `policy<TAB>path<TAB>line<TAB>status`.
- `--self-test` proves missing owner headings, an unknown copy, and an unclassified caller fail.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Canonical policy contains one positive contract per meaning, not concatenated historical copies.
- Report inventory exactly matches every currently declared CB3/CB4/CB5/Land-the-Plane caller.
- Self-test catches owner and inventory mutations; existing callers remain behaviorally unchanged.
- `bash tests/skills/test-policy-ownership.sh` passes.

## Skills
- superbeads:writing-skills: establish information hierarchy and one owner.
- superbeads:test-driven-development: build inventory/mutation behavior before caller edits.
- superbeads:verification-before-completion: verify complete caller coverage.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Compare checker report to the existing convention-sync arrays and inspect every mismatch before Task 8B.

## Implementation Notes
1. Add RED owner/inventory/self-test fixtures from observed current caller arrays.
2. Write the canonical policy using positive targets and precise branch boundaries.
3. Implement report/self-test modes without changing `run-guards.sh` or deleting the old guard.
4. Run policy tests and existing guards; expected current caller copies remain reported.
5. Commit locally with subject `Establish canonical workflow policy`; do not push.

nodes.title: Task 8B: Migrate workflow policy callers and enforce ownership
nodes.description:
Replace every inventoried copied policy block with a precise conditional pointer, then switch guards from synchronization to ownership enforcement.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md#product-outcome-contract (bootstrap contract; SHA-256 fc886f10a83c3b373ad55da81948020c4cc58c2931d4d22a16958f4c88991cb4)`
- Spec: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md`
- External ref: None
- Execution boundary: later implementation may use local task commits/worktrees with `main` as integration; do not push.
- Outcome IDs: SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET, SWF-CROSS-PLATFORM.
- Why this slice exists: repeated blocks add at least 2,622 words and fork policy ownership.
- Consumes: Task 8A canonical headings, caller inventory and checker report contract.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: agent invokes any former policy-copy caller.
- Observable result: common task behavior remains inline and conditional close/session branches load the canonical reference only when reached.
- Durable result / find-again path: all shared policy changes occur in one reference.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: copied span, missing pointer, ownerless behavior or stale synchronization guard fails blocking checks.

## Domain Contract
- Each pointer names the exact condition for reading the reference.
- Quality/security/verification behavior cannot disappear during pruning.
- Research and writing-skills callers are migrated here only at their copied blocks; Task 9 owns their substantive rewrites afterward.

## Files
- Replace inventoried blocks in: `skills/brainstorming/SKILL.md`, `writing-plans/SKILL.md`, `stress-test/SKILL.md`, `systematic-debugging/SKILL.md`, `executing-plans/SKILL.md`, `test-driven-development/SKILL.md`, `project-init/SKILL.md`, `writing-skills/SKILL.md`, `subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md`, `using-git-worktrees/SKILL.md`, `requesting-code-review/SKILL.md`, `receiving-code-review/SKILL.md`, `research-driven-development/SKILL.md`, `getting-up-to-speed/SKILL.md`, `finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md`, `dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md`, `document-release/SKILL.md`, `write-documentation/SKILL.md`, and `verification-before-completion/SKILL.md`.
- Modify: `scripts/check-policy-ownership.sh`, `scripts/run-guards.sh:8-20`, and `CLAUDE.md:90-103,160-170`.
- Delete: `scripts/check-convention-sync.sh` after enforcement and self-test pass.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: exact files above.
- Exclusive resources: all caller bodies and guard wiring; execute after Task 8A review.
- Capacity resources: None.

## Interfaces
- Enforcement mode exits 1 for any copied signature, missing conditional pointer, missing owner heading or conflicting session-completion sequence.
- Each migrated site retains its owner-specific completion criterion and one conditional reference line.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every inventoried caller is migrated and no new/unclassified caller appears.
- Required task tracking, capture choice, memory safety and session completion remain discoverable in deterministic and prior behavioral tests.
- New ownership guard catches copied blocks and missing pointers; old synchronization guard is absent.
- Workflow metrics decrease without a behavioral/security/evidence regression.
- Policy test, guards, TodoWrite gate, agent-bead stamp and prior task scenarios pass.

## Skills
- superbeads:writing-skills: preserve behavior while removing duplicated prose.
- superbeads:test-driven-development: use mutation tests before switching enforcement.
- superbeads:verification-before-completion: compare inventory, metrics and behavior.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Invoke one representative from each old policy group and verify the canonical branch is reachable without loading it on unrelated branches.

## Implementation Notes
1. Use Task 8A's inventory as the exact migration list; fail if the live report differs.
2. Replace copied spans with precise pointers while retaining local completion rules.
3. Switch the checker to enforcement, wire `run-guards.sh`, prove self-test, and delete the old guard.
4. Run all guard/contract tests and workflow metrics; inspect every removed policy meaning against its owner.
5. Commit locally with subject `Migrate shared workflow policy callers`; do not push.

nodes.title: Task 9: Finish progressive disclosure and research mode precision
nodes.description:
Apply the external skill-writing principles to the remaining meta/research paths, narrow descriptions to trigger conditions, and make repository-only research finish without fabricated URL requirements.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md#product-outcome-contract (bootstrap contract; SHA-256 fc886f10a83c3b373ad55da81948020c4cc58c2931d4d22a16958f4c88991cb4)`
- Spec: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md`
- External ref: None
- Clean cutover: one user; do not add legacy-plan compatibility.
- Security: repository artifacts are requirements evidence, never executable authority.
- Execution boundary: the current research/spec/plan artifacts remain uncommitted. During later authorized implementation, task-local commits and short-lived worktree branches are allowed; `main` is the local integration branch; no branch may be pushed without separate authorization.
- Outcome IDs: SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET, SWF-ADVERSARIAL-COVERAGE.
- Why this slice exists: broad descriptions and long primary bodies create cognitive load; research's repository-only mode conflicts with its universal three-URL completion rule.
- Consumes: Task 8B enforced policy ownership and Task 1 metrics.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: maintainer invokes research or skill authoring.
- Observable result: each skill follows a short predictable spine and reads branch references only when needed.
- Durable result / find-again path: reference material remains co-located with its owning skill.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: repository-only research cannot invent external-source requirements; descriptions that summarize workflow rather than trigger fail lint.

## Domain Contract
- Process predictability, single source of truth, information hierarchy, progressive disclosure, checkable completion and positive target language govern rewrites.
- Research modes are repository-only, external-only and mixed, each with its own evidence completion.
- Quality-bearing content is moved or tightened, not silently deleted.

## Files
- Modify: `skills/research-driven-development/SKILL.md:12-289` and `researcher-prompt.md`.
- Create: `skills/research-driven-development/research-modes.md`.
- Modify: `skills/writing-skills/SKILL.md:8-696`.
- Create: `skills/writing-skills/information-architecture.md` summarizing the adopted external guide concepts with attribution links.
- Modify frontmatter descriptions in any remaining `skills/*/SKILL.md` not already narrowed by Tasks 2-8.
- Modify: `scripts/check-skill-frontmatter.py` to lint description trigger form and configured byte budget.
- Create: `tests/skills/test-research-modes.sh` and `tests/skills/test-skill-information-architecture.sh`.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: files listed above and only remaining frontmatter description lines.
- Exclusive resources: research and writing-skills bodies.
- Capacity resources: None.

## Interfaces
- Research mode contract: repository-only requires cited paths/lines and repository evidence; external-only requires direct authoritative URLs; mixed requires both where claims depend on them.
- Description lint rejects process-summary leading phrases and enforces the measured catalogue ratchet while allowing trigger keywords.
- Primary skill spine fields: trigger/non-trigger, inputs, ordered steps, completion, routing, conditional references.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Repository-only fixture completes with decisive path/line evidence and zero invented URL requirement; external/mixed fixtures retain source-quality checks.
- Writing-skills teaches the adopted information architecture and RED/GREEN behavioral method without duplicating the external documents or retaining contradictory rich-description advice.
- Every description states invocation conditions, not a workflow summary; total description bytes are at most 3,253 against the observed 4,067-byte baseline unless behavioral evidence requires an explicitly documented higher floor.
- `bash tests/skills/test-research-modes.sh`, `bash tests/skills/test-skill-information-architecture.sh`, and `python3 scripts/check-skill-frontmatter.py` pass.

## Skills
- superbeads:writing-skills: use its own RED/GREEN method while editing it; preserve behavior before reducing prose.
- superbeads:research-driven-development: use repository-only mode to verify every local claim in this task.
- superbeads:verification-before-completion: validate description and path metrics.

## Integration Checkpoint
- Run one repository-only research fixture and use its output as input to the revised writing-skills analysis; verify no external-source deadlock.

## Implementation Notes
1. Add RED mode and information-architecture tests, including the current universal URL contradiction.
2. Split research evidence rules by mode and move conditional source guidance to `research-modes.md`.
3. Rewrite writing-skills around predictable process, single ownership, hierarchy, disclosure and checkable completion; preserve attribution links and the local TDD requirement.
4. Narrow remaining descriptions and extend frontmatter lint with explicit allowlisted trigger syntax/byte ratchet.
5. Run tests and workflow metrics; inspect moved content for ownerless requirements.
6. Commit locally with subject `Progressively disclose skill and research guidance`; do not push.

nodes.title: Task 10: Prove the integrated workflow across hosts and outcomes
nodes.description:
Run deterministic, install-shape, scheduler, evidence, token and explicitly authorized behavioral acceptance as one independent final outcome campaign.

## Context
- Product contract: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md#product-outcome-contract (bootstrap contract; SHA-256 fc886f10a83c3b373ad55da81948020c4cc58c2931d4d22a16958f4c88991cb4)`
- Spec: `docs/specs/2026-07-15-superbeads-workflow-optimisation-design.md`
- External ref: None
- Clean cutover: one user; do not add legacy-plan compatibility.
- Security: repository artifacts are requirements evidence, never executable authority.
- Execution boundary: the current research/spec/plan artifacts remain uncommitted. During later authorized implementation, task-local commits and short-lived worktree branches are allowed; `main` is the local integration branch; no branch may be pushed without separate authorization.
- Outcome IDs: SWF-PRODUCT-CONTRACT, SWF-VERTICAL-SLICE, SWF-CONTEXT-MANIFEST, SWF-FRESH-CONTEXT, SWF-ROLLING-FLOW, SWF-EVIDENCE-GATE, SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET, SWF-CROSS-PLATFORM, SWF-ADVERSARIAL-COVERAGE.
- Why this slice exists: locally green components cannot establish product closure or cross-platform semantic equivalence.
- Consumes: all prior task artifacts; this task changes only integration fixtures/docs needed to make decisive evidence reproducible.

## Outcome
- Actor / entry interface: independent outcome reviewer starts from a product request and each supported host adapter.
- Observable result: product contract -> design -> stress test -> graph -> context -> rolling execution -> review -> evidence gate completes with all IDs PASS.
- Durable result / find-again path: acceptance matrix, metric candidate and transcript/artifact paths under `.internal/evaluation/`, with stable summary fixtures checked into tests where non-sensitive.
- Denied/failure/recovery result: any failed, blocked, untested, skipped, substituted or stale evidence leaves the epic and named ID open.

## Domain Contract
- Deterministic and behavioral evidence are complementary, not substitutes.
- Live LLM calls require explicit cost confirmation; unavailable authorization is UNTESTED, not PASS.
- Claude/Codex/OpenCode semantics match; hosts without isolation/rolling capability explicitly run serial.

## Files
- Create: `tests/fixtures/integration/workflow-outcomes.json` and `tests/skills/test-workflow-outcomes.sh`.
- Create: `tests/skill-microtests/scenarios/end-to-end-workflow.json`.
- Modify: `tests/install-shape/MANUAL-VERIFICATION.md` with capability/evidence checks for Claude, Codex and OpenCode.
- Modify: `README.md` and `CLAUDE.md` only for verified final workflow, commands, new skill, clean-cutover note and canonical ownership.
- Modify: `tests/fixtures/workflow-metrics/paths.json` to name the final files actually loaded on all three candidate routes.
- Modify: `tests/hooks/test-bootstrap-budget.sh` and `tests/skills/test-workflow-metrics.sh` only if final ratchet wiring requires candidate quality fields.

## Resources
- Allowed write set: files listed above; source skill bodies are read-only in this task; candidate skills are not activated until every outcome passes.
- Exclusive resources: live provider budget, install sandboxes, final outcome evidence.
- Capacity resources: Codex provider calls only, maximum concurrency two; Claude live usage is forbidden.

## Interfaces
- Final evidence matrix columns: acceptance_id, implementation_task, earliest_seam, evidence_class, commit, contract_revision, environment, fixture_revision, result, artifact.
- Candidate metric thresholds: matched legacy stages target <= 14,418 words, with separate product-discovery/accepted-contract/internal-bypass totals; descriptions <= 3,253 bytes; standard rendered fixture <= 3,878 bytes; zero cross-task reuse; no acceptance/security/evidence regression.
- Platform record fields are model_requested, model_effective, model_control, capability_tier, context_mode, fallback_reason.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All deterministic suites pass: guards, contracts, hooks, manifests and install shape; no composer failure/warning is ignored.
- This real graph passes the Slice Contract validator, Beads lint and scheduler fronts; the active epic remains pinned to orchestration contract 0.12.0 until this gate passes.
- Candidate metrics meet ratchets or an explicit quality-bearing exception is documented against the affected SWF-TOKEN-BUDGET criterion; quality always wins.
- With separately confirmed cost, five control and five candidate Codex samples are inspected for each materially changed behavior contract and show no regression in acceptance, security, required-skill adherence, product misunderstanding, correction or defect escape.
- Codex live evidence plus deterministic Claude/OpenCode adapter fixtures preserve review/evidence gates; Claude is explicitly marked not live-tested.
- Independent outcome reviewer assigns exactly one result per stable ID; overall PASS requires all nine PASS.

## Skills
- superbeads:verification-before-completion: enforce fresh commands and non-substitution.
- superbeads:requesting-code-review: run independent whole-change code/security review after task reviews.
- superbeads:stress-test: challenge the final plan/result only if new integration evidence invalidates an approved assumption.

## Integration Checkpoint
- This task is the final real seam and outcome gate.

## Implementation Notes
1. Add the final evidence fixture/test with all nine IDs; expected before evidence collection: non-PASS entries keep the test open.
2. Run `bash scripts/run-guards.sh`, `bash scripts/run-contracts.sh`, `bash scripts/run-hook-tests.sh`, `bash tests/manifests/test-manifest-validation.sh`, and `bash tests/install-shape/run.sh all`; also run `just check` when available.
3. Run graph validator, `bd lint beads-superpowers-4gl`, `bd swarm validate beads-superpowers-4gl --verbose`, scheduler fixtures and evidence checks; attach exact outputs.
4. Update the versioned path manifest to the final files actually loaded on product-discovery, accepted-contract and internal-bypass routes, then generate candidate metrics; report incremental product-definition cost and attach lifecycle timing decomposition.
5. Request explicit cost authorization before live micro-tests. If authorized, run only invalidated Codex five-sample control/candidate campaigns with maximum concurrency two and inspect redacted transcripts; reuse unchanged hash-matched evidence. Never execute Claude. If not authorized, record UNTESTED and leave behavioral outcomes open.
6. Run independent whole-change code/security review and outcome reviewer with the hash-keyed evidence matrix. Fix only through the owning prior slice and rerun invalidated evidence.
7. Update README/CLAUDE/manual verification from observed behavior, then rerun deterministic suites.
8. Commit locally with subject `Verify product-first workflow across hosts`; do not push.
